steps.title: Find the main verb later
steps.content: The subject is John Donne's works, and the main verb appears later: had been essentially gathering dust. The phrase much admired during his lifetime and in the decades that followed interrupts the subject and main verb as a nonessential modifier.

steps.title: Open the interrupting phrase
steps.content: Choice A and Choice B insert the extra verb were, which clashes with the later main verb. Choice D repeats had been and fails to open the interruption. The blank needs works followed by a comma so the modifier is set off before the sentence resumes. The answer is C.
